dialogos.texto_en: *yawn*
(Last night, I was up late writing a letter.)
(Thanks to that, I'm sleep-deprived,\n but I can't nap in school...)
(But, I didn't want to keep Futaro waiting any longer,\n so I had to finish writing.)
(We talk on the phone almost every day,\n and exchange messages on social media.)
(But writing a letter\n felt completely different from that,\n it was enjoyable.)
(You can only write so much in a letter, \n but I had a lot to say.\n So much that it took me ages to write them.)
(I wonder if Futaro felt the same \n when he wrote the letters...)
Ah, that was a close one.\nI was just about to walk past the postbox.
Well, I'll do a final check before I mail the postcard.
(I checked many times at home...\n If something happens and it's delayed\n or doesn't arrive, that'd be troublesome.)
The stamp is attached,\nand the address is...correct.
The recipient, Uesugi Futaro.
Okay, good to go%p-2;――
Oh no, I forgot!\nThe address on the back and my name...\nYes, now it's okay.
I hope I sealed it properly.
(Futaro will be surprised to receive\n such a thick envelope.)
(I was worried if I could seal it properly.)
(Because it's Futaro,\n he might write an even longer letter.)
(When will I get a reply?\n I hope it comes soon.)
(How long does it take for a letter to arrive?\n It's probably not tomorrow,\n so maybe the day after tomorrow...)
(Futaro will surely read it, and then write a reply\n on the same day.)
(If he mails it in the postbox the next day,\n I might receive it on the weekend.)
(Oh, but he might be busy with college\n and won't have time to read it right away.\n He also said he started working part-time.)
(...I hope he isn't overworking.\n Is he eating properly?)
(When we talked on the phone, he seemed fine, though%p-2;――%p;)
(Wh-what am I doing?\n I'm standing alone in front of the postbox...)
Alright, finally%p-2;――
Yes, it's perfect.
Alright, I'm off.
...Teehee.
(Hm? A call from Nino?)
I'm going to school first.]
Ah...
so I left home earlier.]
See you at school, then.]
yeah
...Alright.\nWell then, let's do our best today.
Futaro, do your best, too.
